questions.text: I sense when a compliment is actually passive-aggressive.
I rarely feel that my emotions are too much for me to handle.
I hold the view that the best thing I can do is become the strongest version of myself.
When I see an opportunity, I move quickly to claim it.
I feel that healthy self-focus gives me the strength to show up fully in everything I do.
I feel that the moment I stop giving is the moment I stop living fully.
I feel that the moral imperative to address suffering grows stronger the more clearly I understand what someone is going through.
I believe that not everything worth doing can or should be measured.
I find that self-imposed deadlines feel artificial and don't motivate me.
I prefer to get buy-in before acting on behalf of the group.
I find that I can engage with the most difficult aspects of the human condition without it affecting my capacity to function.
I find social situations easier when expectations are made explicit.
I often do not notice when people are speaking in code around me.
I find that sticking to a proven methodology gives me confidence in uncertain situations.
I sometimes need to physically move or pace to discharge the intensity of what I'm feeling.
I rarely redefine objectives that were set by the group.
I believe that emotional composure during a crisis is not a luxury but a necessity for clear and fair decision-making.
I can be in the midst of uncertainty and still feel fundamentally okay.
